title: What Next.js Is and Why It Exists

learningObjectives.text: Explain full-stack react framework using a new example and justify the result.
Explain app router mental model using a new example and justify the result.
Explain framework responsibilities using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: This topic turns what next.js is and why it exists from isolated terminology into a practical tool for reading, writing, and reviewing code. It belongs to the **Rendering Foundations** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Full-stack React framework** → **App Router mental model** → **Framework responsibilities**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Full-stack React framework
sections.body: Next.js adds routing, server rendering, data conventions, asset optimization, build tooling, and deployment integration around React.

sections.title: App Router mental model
sections.body: Folders and special files define route segments, pages, layouts, loading UI, errors, and handlers. Server Components are the default.

sections.title: Framework responsibilities
sections.body: The framework coordinates routing, rendering, bundling, caching, and deployment, while the application still owns domain and security decisions.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: The App Router maps the file to a route and a Server Component loads data.

commonMistakes: Marking a large route tree as client code when only a small interaction needs it.
Confusing React Server Components with server-side rendering.
Assuming cached data is fresh without an explicit policy and invalidation path.
Placing security-sensitive validation only in the client or middleware layer.
summary: What Next.js Is and Why It Exists connects Full-stack React framework, App Router mental model, Framework responsibilities.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

assessmentProfile.label: Conceptual and mental-model review
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic is primarily definitions, mental models, comparisons, or framework purpose. Scenario MCQs are more appropriate than forced code trivia.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
